  │

  │     Goal: ≥4.0  (≥8.0 ideal)   Actual: 3.190
  │
  │  ✅ Regime Robustness  —  8/8
  │  decay=10.1% CAGR ratio=0.87 ΔSharpe=-0.22 — the edge
  │  held into the second half of the dataset. No evidence of
  │  time-specific overfitting.
  │     Goal: decay<30%, CAGR ratio≥0.60   Actual: decay=10.1%  CAGR ratio=0.87  ΔSharpe=-0.22

  ┌─ SUPPORTING METRICS  (18/23)  Grade: B+ ──────────────────
  │
  │  ✅ Neighbor Plateau (Parameter Robustness)  —  8/8
  │  100.0% of neighbors within 0.5 Sharpe — parameters sit
  │  on a broad stable plateau. Operational imprecision is
  │  tolerable.
  │     Goal: ≥70%  (≥80% ideal)   Actual: 100.0%
  │
  │  ✅ MC Reshuffle % Losing  —  8/8
  │  0.00% of 2,000 reshuffles lost money — positive
  │  expectation is robust to path ordering.
  │     Goal: <1%  (<0.5% ideal)   Actual: 0.00%
  │
  │  ⚠  Slippage Resilience  —  2/7
  │  Sharpe 1.959 at 2× slippage — fragile to cost increases.
  │  Backtest slippage assumptions may be optimistic.
  │     Goal: Sharpe≥1.8 at 2× slippage  (≥2.5 ideal)   Actual: 1.959
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU HAVE — DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST ─────────────────
  │  ✅ DSR and MTL — statistical validity proven (90.58% DSR)
  │  ✅ MC reshuffle (2,000 iterations) and block bootstrap stress tests
  │  ✅ Neighbor plateau test — joint parameter robustness confirmed
  │  ✅ Slippage, cost curve, and capacity curve — operational concerns addressed
  │  ✅ Shock injection — tail risk quantified under artificial stress scenarios
  │  ✅ Drawdown episode analysis — depth, duration, and recovery tracked
  │  ✅ Regime robustness — first-half vs second-half performance split
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ BOTTOM LINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  │  The strategy scores 54/100. There are 1 outright
  │  failures and 4 marginal results that require attention
  │  before this is deployable. Failing metrics: Walk-Forward
  │  Stability (CV). Focus on the gating metrics first —
  │  Sharpe and DSR must both clear before the other scores
  │  matter.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

│  Mean Sharpe:    2.812  (±2.834)
│  Mean OOS DSR:   78.1%  ⚠ FAIL
│  % folds positive Sharpe: 89%
│  Stability (CV=1.008): ⚠ UNSTABLE
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  INSTITUTIONAL SCORECARD
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Overall Grade:  D  —  Not Deployable — Fundamental Issues
  Total Score:    46 / 100  (4 pass  4 marginal  2 fail)

  ┌─ GATING METRICS  (21/45)  Grade: D ────────────────────
  │
  │  ⚠  Deflated Sharpe Ratio (DSR)  —  6/15
  │  DSR 89.58% — fails the statistical validity gate. The
  │  backtest Sharpe has a meaningful probability of being a
  │  search artifact.
  │     Goal: ≥95%  (≥99.5% ideal)   Actual: 89.58%
  │
  │  ❌ Walk-Forward Stability (CV)  —  0/15
  │  CV=1.01 89% folds positive 1 unstable — high
  │  instability. The strategy's edge may be period-specific
  │  rather than durable.
  │     Goal: CV<0.25  (CV<0.15 ideal)   Actual: CV=1.01  89% folds positive  1 unstable
  │
  │  ✅ Maximum Drawdown  —  15/15
  │  MaxDD -68.68% — inside the institutional mandate.
  │  Drawdown discipline is clean.
  │     Goal: ≤30%  (≤25% ideal)   Actual: -68.68%
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ CORE METRICS  (7/32)  Grade: D ───────────────────────
  │
  │  ⚠  Sharpe Ratio  —  1/8
  │  Sharpe 1.945 — below institutional standard.
  │  Risk-adjusted returns need improvement.
  │     Goal: ≥2.0  (≥3.0 ideal)   Actual: 1.945
  │
  │  ✅ Calmar Ratio  —  4/8
  │  Calmar 7.23 — marginal. Either CAGR needs to rise or
  │  MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │     Goal: ≥3.0  (≥10.0 ideal)   Actual: 7.23
  │
  │  ⚠  Sortino Ratio  —  1/8
  │  Sortino 3.493 — downside volatility is too high relative
  │  to returns. Distribution needs improvement.
  │     Goal: ≥4.0  (≥8.0 ideal)   Actual: 3.493
  │
  │  ❌ Regime Robustness  —  1/8
  │  decay=30.0% CAGR ratio=0.29 ΔSharpe=-0.74 — significant
  │  performance decay in OOS half. This is the most
  │  important gap to investigate before deployment.
  │     Goal: decay<30%, CAGR ratio≥0.60   Actual: decay=30.0%  CAGR ratio=0.29  ΔSharpe=-0.74
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ SUPPORTING METRICS  (18/23)  Grade: B+ ──────────────────
  │
  │  ✅ Neighbor Plateau (Parameter Robustness)  —  8/8
  │  100.0% of neighbors within 0.5 Sharpe — parameters sit
  │  on a broad stable plateau. Operational imprecision is
  │  tolerable.
  │     Goal: ≥70%  (≥80% ideal)   Actual: 100.0%
  │
  │  ✅ MC Reshuffle % Losing  —  8/8
  │  0.00% of 2,000 reshuffles lost money — positive
  │  expectation is robust to path ordering.
  │     Goal: <1%  (<0.5% ideal)   Actual: 0.00%
  │
  │  ⚠  Slippage Resilience  —  2/7
  │  Sharpe 1.891 at 2× slippage — fragile to cost increases.
  │  Backtest slippage assumptions may be optimistic.
  │     Goal: Sharpe≥1.8 at 2× slippage  (≥2.5 ideal)   Actual: 1.891
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU HAVE — DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST ─────────────────
  │  ✅ DSR and MTL — statistical validity proven (89.58% DSR)
  │  ✅ MC reshuffle (2,000 iterations) and block bootstrap stress tests
  │  ✅ Neighbor plateau test — joint parameter robustness confirmed
  │  ✅ Slippage, cost curve, and capacity curve — operational concerns addressed
  │  ✅ Shock injection — tail risk quantified under artificial stress scenarios
  │  ✅ Drawdown episode analysis — depth, duration, and recovery tracked
  │  ✅ Regime robustness — first-half vs second-half performance split
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ BOTTOM LINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  │  The strategy scores 46/100. There are 2 outright
  │  failures and 4 marginal results that require attention
  │  before this is deployable. Failing metrics: Walk-Forward
  │  Stability (CV), Regime Robustness. Focus on the gating
  │  metrics first — Sharpe and DSR must both clear before
  │  the other scores matter.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

--- A - No Filter ---
"None of [DatetimeIndex(['2025-02-13', '2025-02-14', '2025-02-15', '2025-02-16',\n               '2025-02-17', '2025-02-18', '2025-02-19', '2025-02-20',\n               '2025-02-21', '2025-02-22',\n               ...\n               '2026-03-10', '2026-03-11', '2026-03-12', '2026-03-13',\n               '2026-03-14', '2026-03-15', '2026-03-16', '2026-03-17',\n               '2026-03-18', '2026-03-19'],\n              dtype='datetime64[us]', length=398, freq=None)] are in the [index]"

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  STEP 6 — PBO  (Probability of Backtest Overfitting)
  CSCV  ·  Bailey, Borwein, Lopez de Prado & Zhu (2015)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  PBO — PROBABILITY OF BACKTEST OVERFITTING  [All filter modes]
  CSCV method · Bailey et al. (2015) · J. Computational Finance
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Strategies (N)        : 2
  Trading days (T)      : 398  (sub-period len=24d, S=16)
  Combinations C(S,S/2) : 12,870

  PBO                   : 0.909  (90.9%)
  Grade                 : ⚠  LOW RESOLUTION (N=2 < 10 — see warnings)
  Performance Degradation (PD) : +0.4737  Sharpe drop train→test
  Probability of Loss (POL)    : 0.063  (6.3%)  (test Sharpe of winner < 0)
  Discrete logit values (2) : -1.0986  +1.0986

  ── Training-window win counts ──
  A - No Filter                              6898×  ( 53.6%)  ██████████████████████████
  A - Tail Guardrail                         5972×  ( 46.4%)  ███████████████████████

  ── Logit(ω) distribution (λ_c = log(rank/(N−rank))) ──
  Mean  : -0.8982    Median: -1.0986
  Stdev : 0.6326     Range : [-1.0986, +1.0986]
  λ < 0 : 11696 / 12870  = PBO
  λ = 0 :    0 / 12870  (winner ranked at exact median)
  λ > 0 : 1174 / 12870

  ── Warnings ──
  ⚠  LOW RESOLUTION: N=2 strategies means logit(ω) can only take 2
       discrete values (-1.0986, +1.0986). PBO=0.909 reflects which of
       these 2 buckets the training winner falls into — not a
       continuous probability. Grade bands are not reliable at N < 10.

  ⚠  STRUCTURAL BIAS: when the N strategies are filter variants of
       the same base strategy, unfiltered variants have more active
       trading days per sub-period, which mechanically inflates their
       sub-period Sharpe. This causes unfiltered variants to win more
       training splits even when filtered variants are stronger over
       the full period. Interpret win counts with this in mind.

  ── Interpretation ──
  With N=2 strategies the logit(ω) distribution has only 2
  discrete values, so PBO grade bands (Low / Moderate / High) are
  not statistically meaningful here.
  
  What this result DOES tell you:
    · In 91% of train/test splits the filter with the best
      in-sample Sharpe did NOT rank in the top half out-of-sample.
      → Filter selection by in-sample Sharpe alone is unstable.
    · The unfiltered variant dominates training splits because it
      has more active trading days per sub-period, mechanically
      inflating its sub-period Sharpe (active-day structural bias).
    · This is expected behaviour, not evidence of overfitting.
  
  What this result does NOT tell you:
    · It does NOT mean the underlying strategy is overfit.
    · POL = 6% means the training winner ALWAYS had positive
      test Sharpe — the strategy was profitable in every OOS window.
  
  Recommended action:
    · To get a meaningful PBO, run CSCV across a large grid of
      independently-tuned parameter configurations (N ≥ 20 ideally),
      not across filter variants of the same base strategy.
    · Use POL and PD as the primary overfitting diagnostics here.
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  PBO chart saved: audit_outputs_BEFORE/run_20260331_075108/pbo_cscv_All_filter_modes.png

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  REGIME FILTER COMPARISON - BEST_SHARPE CONFIG
  Note: CAGR and Sharpe annualised over TOTAL elapsed days (incl. flat).
  Flat days count as 0% return. This prevents filtered runs from
  appearing better merely because they traded fewer days.
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
